rulings.Case Title: VLADIMIR MIROV VS. ARKADY BERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Alec Bergers Application For Writ Of Execution Against Real Property (148 Amber Street); For The Assignment Of Rents And For The Appointment Of A Receiver
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 10, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT ALEC BERGER's Motion For Alec Bergers Application For Writ Of Execution Against Real Property (148 Amber Street); For The Assignment Of Rents And For The Appointment Of A Receiver The motion is denied. Mr. Varum's son is not a judgment debtor in this action. The Court cannot make a determination in the context of this motion about whether the transfer of rights from Mr. Varum to his son was fraudulent. Rather, the Uniform Fraudulent Transfer Act requires a separate action to set aside a fraudulent transfer. (See Civ. Code sec. 3439.07(a).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: SCHAPIRO-THORN INC., VS. DANIEL L. MITCHELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 10, 2015, Line 14, CROSS DEFENDANT SUZIE S. THORN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. When a plaintiff makes allegations against an employee who was not a party to an agreement which includes an arbitration provision and the employee is inextricably intertwined with these obligations, the employee is entitled to the benefits of the arbitration provision. (Goldman v. KPMG LLP (2009) 173 Cal. App.4th 209; Dryer v. Los Angeles Rams (1985) 40 Cal. 3d 406, 418.) Here, the Cross-Complaint states: "[a]t all times herein mentioned," Ms. Thorn "was the agent and employee" of Shapiro-Thorn, Inc. "[A]t all times herein mentioned," Ms. Thorn "was¿ acting within the scope of said agency and employment." (Cross-Complaint 2.) All of the allegations included in the Cross-Complaint arose from the scope of Ms. Thorn's employment by Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Shapiro-Thorn, Inc. Therefore, because all claims in the Cross-Complaint were denied with respect to Shapiro-Thorn, Inc., Ms. Thorn, as an employee of Shapiro-Thorn, Inc., is entitled to the benefit of the arbitration award providing that all of Mr. Mitchell's claims against Shapiro-Thorn are denied. Therefore the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: LINDSEY E. SCULLY VS. MENLO CAPITAL GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses From Menlo Defendants To Form Interrogatories Set Two And Requests For Sanctions Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 10, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF LINDSEY SCULLY'ss Motion To Compel Further Responses From Menlo Defendants To Form Interrogatories Set Two And Requests For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to September 17, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.

rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Contempt Sanctions Against Martin Eng, Lombard Flats, Llc And Michael Yesk
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 10, 2015, Line 9, PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC'S Motion For Contempt Sanctions Against Martin Eng, Lombard Flats, LLC And Michael Yesk. The motion is granted in part. The court strikes the complaint in #545051 (Lombard Flats LLC v. Legal Recovery, LLC) as violating CCP 391 et seq. The court previously declared that defendant Eng was a vexatious litigant. Defendant Eng cannot circumvent CCP 391 by suing in the name of his alter ego LLC and employing a strawman attorney. The court notes that that the complaint in #545051 (Lombard Flats LLC v. Legal Recovery, LLC) is in the name of the LLC even though the gravamen of the complaint attacks promissory notes that defendant Eng allegedly signed as the borrower. The remainder of the motion is denied. Plaintiff may employ collection measures other than contempt to collect on the prior sanctions orders. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to September 17, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EG)
